params.description: The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14060, 14061, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for a given game and competition. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: Pre-Game and Period Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14060, 14061, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns the game odds for a given game and competition. In this endpoint both full-game and partial-game odds are included. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL p

info.name: In-Game Odds Line Movement with Resulting [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of a Soccer game. GameIDs can be found in the Games API. Valid entries are 14060, 14061, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns in-play game odds (spread, moneyline, total) for a given game in a competition. This also includes Resulting: for markets with a ResultType, each line will be graded and it will be determined whether the bet would have won or lost. Returns the full line movement for the given game. This endpoint has a longer cache as it is meant for historical data/line movement rather than the most up to the second line. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: season
params.description:
Year of the season
Examples: 2020, 2021, etc.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns available Futures markets for the given season. Does not include line movement. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: marketId
params.description: The MarketId of the desired market for which to pull all outcomes/bets.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns full line movement for a given BettingMarket. Due to the sheer size of the output and the level of detail, it is intended for historical data purposes and not for the most up-to-the-second lines. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns the markets and available outcomes for a given BettingEventID. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: gameid
params.description: The GameID of the desired game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns the markets of all available types (e.g. Player Props, Team Props) and available outcomes for a given GameID in a competition. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: Betting Markets - by Market Type [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: eventId
params.description: The EventId of the desired event/game for which to pull all betting markets (includes outcomes/bets).

params.name: marketTypeID
params.description: The Market Type ID of the desired MarketTypes to pull. Some common types include: 1 for Game Lines, 2 for Player Props, 3 for Team Props, 6 for Game Props

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: Returns Markets and available outcomes for a given event and market type requested. A lighter call than by BettingEventID as it only includes markets tagged with the specific MarketType, a full list of which is available for each sport in its Betting Metadata endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.

info.name: Betting Player Props - by Game [Sportsbook Group]

params.name: format
params.description: Desired response format. Valid entries are JSON or XML.

params.name: competition
params.description: An indication of a soccer competition/league. This value can be the CompetitionId or the Competition Key. Possible values include: EPL, 1, MLS, 8, etc.

params.name: gameId
params.description: The unique GameID of the game in question.

params.name: sportsbookgroup
params.description:
The name of the Sportsbook grouping.
Examples: G1100

items.docs: This works in the same way as Betting Markets by Market Type but is prefiltered to the Player Props type only. Ideal if your application will only ever require Player Props, but if you also want Team Props etc. it is recommended to use the by Market Type endpoint. A Sportsbook Group must be specified as a URL parameter.
